method_atom: a dynamical-systems analysis of a very high-dimensional deterministic nonlinear system: computing its unstable invariant solutions (equilibria and their leading eigenvalues and eigenfunctions), globally continuing the associated unstable invariant manifolds, and building a low-dimensional projection that visualizes how these manifolds partition the region of state space that transiently chaotic trajectories explore, with symmetry-induced heteroclinic connections organizing the geometry.
object_atom: the velocity field of an incompressible viscous fluid sheared between two moving walls (plane Couette flow) at moderate Reynolds number in a small periodic cell, governed by the Navier-Stokes equations.
brief_reason: the invariant-solution / unstable-manifold / heteroclinic-tessellation machinery in a high-dimensional state space is transferable to any nonlinear system, while the sheared viscous fluid governed by Navier-Stokes is the fluid-dynamics-specific object.
